title: Cocos2d-x NavMesh Bootstrap (NavMeshQueryReplay)
summary: A two-phase task: Phase A restores a hollowed Recast/Detour navmesh integration in the cocos2d-x C++ engine (geometry load, tile/crowd setup, agent and obstacle components, pathfinding, and debug rendering). Phase B implements a deterministic Python replay harness (NavMeshQueryReplay) that executes fixed-tick navigation queries and records a reproducible event stream and aggregate state hash. The two subsystems are independently testable and have no cross-language code dependencies.
instructionPreview: You are given: - A cocos2d-x source tree at /workspace/cocos2d-x - A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (NavMeshQueryReplay) - Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets - Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts
instruction:
You are given:
- A cocos2d-x source tree at /workspace/cocos2d-x
- A deterministic headless mini-game project at /workspace/game_project (NavMeshQueryReplay)
- Replay and expected-output assets under /workspace/assets
- Helper scripts under /workspace/scripts

NavMeshQueryReplay runs fixed-tick navigation queries (path requests, navmesh
sampling, and obstacle-filter toggles) and records a deterministic event stream
plus final aggregate state hash.

Your task is to complete a full bootstrap workflow:

Phase A - Engine repair and verification
1) Restore heavily hollowed Recast/Detour navmesh integration: geometry load,
   tile/crowd setup, agent sync/move, steering, and obstacle registration paths
   are broken or stubbed. Multiple subtle correctness bugs are also planted on
   the same call chain (parameter swaps, shortcut repair, distance checks).
   Validate behavior end-to-end; do not assume a single localized edit is enough.
2) Build cocos2d-x:
     /workspace/scripts/build_cocos2dx.sh
3) Run focused engine checks:
     /workspace/scripts/run_engine_checks.sh

Phase B - Game repair and verification
4) Implement game scripts under /workspace/game_project/scripts so replay execution
   produces correct outputs from consistent navmesh query-chain behavior.
5) Run the game twice with same replay and seed:
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_1 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
     RUN_DIR=/workspace/output/replay_run_2 /workspace/scripts/run_headless_replay.sh
6) Copy first run as canonical game output:
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
     cp /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256 /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
7) Write /workspace/output/determinism_report.json with:
     same_seed_hash_equal, run_1_hash, run_2_hash,
     run_1_event_lines, run_2_event_lines
     (derive hashes from your own replay outputs; no reference SHA256 is shipped in the workspace.)

Required output files:
- /workspace/output/engine_checks.json
- /workspace/output/engine_test_stdout.log
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/nav_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_1/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.json
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/nav_events.jsonl
- /workspace/output/replay_run_2/engine_trace.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.json
- /workspace/output/game_final_state.sha256
- /workspace/output/determinism_report.json

Rules:
- Do not edit files under /tests.
- Do not fabricate hashes; compute them from the actual JSON outputs you generate.
- Do not bypass engine build/check scripts.
- Do not replace navmesh query logic with mocks or stubs.

moduleDag.description: A two-phase task: Phase A restores a hollowed Recast/Detour navmesh integration in the cocos2d-x C++ engine (geometry load, tile/crowd setup, agent and obstacle components, pathfinding, and debug rendering). Phase B implements a deterministic Python replay harness (NavMeshQueryReplay) that executes fixed-tick navigation queries and records a reproducible event stream and aggregate state hash. The two subsystems are independently testable and have no cross-language code dependencies.

nodes.label: NavMesh Utility Layer
nodes.description: Low-level utility primitives shared across the navmesh subsystem: LinearAllocator (bump allocator for Detour tile cache, including resize for buffer management), FastLZCompressor (compress/decompress tile data), MeshProcess (poly area flags and off-mesh connection injection into nav params), and path corridor helpers (getSteerTarget, fixupCorridor, fixupShortcuts, inRange).

nodes.label: NavMesh Debug Draw
nodes.description: GPU debug rendering subsystem that batches duDebugDraw primitive calls into cocos2d-x CustomCommand draw calls with a shared vertex buffer. Implements begin/end/vertex, depth masking, RGBA color conversion, primitive type mapping, renderer state save/restore in before/after visit callbacks, and clear() for vertex/primitive list cleanup.

nodes.label: NavMesh Agent Component
nodes.description: Scene component binding a cocos2d-x Node to a dtCrowdAgent. Implements addTo/removeFrom the crowd, onEnter/onExit scene callbacks that register with the NavMesh, move/stop/ pause/resume/completeOffMeshLink state machine, preUpdate (crowd target dispatch) and postUpdate (position sync), syncToNode/syncToAgent transforms, and fixes the planted radius/height parameter swap in convertTodtAgentParam.

nodes.label: NavMesh Obstacle Component
nodes.description: Scene component registering a dynamic cylinder with dtTileCache. Implements addTo/removeFrom the tile cache (with radius/height bug fix), removeFrom for obstacle deregistration, onEnter/onExit lifecycle, preUpdate/postUpdate sync, syncToNode (position read-back), and syncToObstacle (re-registration on transform change, with the planted AND→OR position-test bug fix).

nodes.label: NavMesh Path Follower
nodes.description: Path corridor wrapper with smooth steering, turn prediction, and off-mesh handling. Manages a dtPathCorridor and corner buffer, provides init/reset/setTarget/update loop, findCorners, topology and visibility optimization, constrained movePosition/moveTargetPosition, off-mesh connection traversal, corridor validity checks, trimInvalidPath recovery, computeSteeringDirection (toward next corner), computeDesiredSpeed (deceleration near corners), predictTurnAngle (angle between segments), and getDistanceToTarget.

nodes.label: NavMesh Query Helper
nodes.description: Extended query utilities wrapping dtNavMeshQuery for common operations: findNearestPoint, sampleRandomPoint/InCircle, raycast with hit interpolation, findPolysAroundCircle/Shape, getPolyHeight, closestPointOnPoly/Boundary, findDistanceToWall, isPositionOnNavMesh (threshold check), getPathDistance (segment sum), smoothPath (Douglas-Peucker simplification), interpolatePath (parametric t along path), findStraightPath, batchRaycast (loop over multiple queries), and findLocalNeighbourhood.

nodes.label: NavMesh Crowd Manager
nodes.description: Higher-level crowd management wrapper: dtCrowd allocation with obstacle avoidance presets, agent add/remove/updateParameters, requestMoveTarget/Velocity/reset, per-frame crowd update, agent access (const and editable), active agent counting, group assignment with formation configs (LINE/COLUMN/WEDGE/CIRCLE), updateFormationTargets (perpendicular offset calculation with navmesh projection), avoidance config, query filter/area cost setup, position/velocity/desiredVelocity queries, active/state checks, findNearestAgent proximity search, and getAgentPath corridor copy.

nodes.label: NavMesh Tile Manager
nodes.description: Tile cache management: dtTileCache creation with params/alloc/comp/proc, buildTileAtPosition (world-to-tile coordinate mapping), addTile/removeTile, cylinder and box obstacle management with pending-update tracking, multi-update-per-frame loop, tile count/obstacle count queries, getTileAt (header-based tx/ty match), getObstacleByRef, queryTiles (bounding-box tile enumeration), buildNavMeshTilesAt, calcTightTileBounds, compressTile/decompressTile (allocator-backed buffer management), accessor methods, and processPendingUpdates.

nodes.label: NavMesh Debug Utilities
nodes.description: Extended debug visualization helpers: drawNavMeshPoly (single polygon triangulation), drawNavMeshPolyBoundaries (inner/outer boundary edges), drawNavMeshPolysWithFlags (flagged polygon filtering), drawNavMeshNodes (query graph node pool visualization), drawNavMeshBVTree (AABB tree boxes), drawTileCacheLayer (heightfield cells), drawTileCacheObstacles (cylinder/box obstacles), drawCrowdAgent (circle+velocity arrow), drawCrowdAgentCorners (path corner markers), drawCrowdAgentTrail (movement history), drawPath (line strip), drawPathCorridor (corridor polygon highlighting), drawStraightPath (waypoint path), drawCostHeatmap (Dijkstra-based cost gradient), drawOffMeshConnections (arc visualization), drawQueryStats (overlay bar charts), collectQueryStats (node pool stats collection), drawAreaCosts (per-area coloring), drawAgentNeighbours (proximity lines), drawVelocityObstacles (RVO sampling visualization).

nodes.label: NavMesh Core
nodes.description: Top-level navmesh object owning dtNavMesh, dtTileCache, dtCrowd, and dtNavMeshQuery. Implements parseRow (geometry file line parser), initWithFilePath (path storage and initialization dispatch), destructor (resource cleanup for dt objects, allocator, compressor, meshProcess, and agent/obstacle lists), loadGeomFile (off-mesh connection parsing), loadNavMeshFile (tile-cache deserialization using LinearAllocator/ FastLZCompressor/MeshProcess from utils), agent and obstacle registry (addNavMeshAgent/Obstacle calling agent->addTo and obstacle->addTo), per-frame update loop (agent/obstacle preUpdate → crowd.update → tileCache.update → postUpdate), smooth-path findPath (using path corridor helpers from utils), and debug draw dispatch through NavMeshDebugDraw.

nodes.label: Game NavMesh Contract
nodes.description: Shared constants and factory for the Python replay harness: EVENT_TYPES mapping (path/sample/obstacle/raycast query strings to canonical event type names), QUERY_COSTS (per-event-type cost weights), REQUIRED_TRACE_FLAGS (boolean flags the engine trace dict must carry), and make_trace() factory that initializes the trace dict with all required keys and flags set to their initial values.

nodes.label: Game Replay I/O
nodes.description: File I/O utilities for the replay harness: load_replay reads a JSON replay spec, write_json creates parent directories and writes sorted/indented JSON, write_jsonl creates parent directories and writes one sorted JSON object per line. Has no imports from other game scripts.

nodes.label: Game Simulation World
nodes.description: Provides build_trace(), which constructs the initial engine_trace dict for a replay run by delegating to make_trace() from navmesh_contract and augmenting it with tick_dt and planned_query_count fields from the replay spec.

nodes.label: Game NavMesh Simulation System
nodes.description: Core replay simulation loop: run_nav_replay() iterates ticks, processes rule switches (obstacle toggle), executes query items (path/sample/obstacle/raycast) against spawned agents using a SHA256-seeded deterministic hit function, performs distance-based raycast hit detection, accumulates per-type and per-agent hit counts, emits a structured nav_events list, and returns the final_state aggregate dict. Imports EVENT_TYPES from navmesh_contract.

edges.label: NavMesh.cpp instantiates LinearAllocator, FastLZCompressor, and MeshProcess in loadNavMeshFile, and calls getSteerTarget/fixupCorridor/fixupShortcuts/inRange inside findPath
NavMesh.cpp holds a NavMeshDebugDraw member and dispatches all duDebugDraw calls through it in dtDraw(), drawAgents(), drawObstacles(), and drawOffMeshConnections()

NavMesh.cpp::addNavMeshAgent calls agent->addTo(_crowed) and the per-frame update loop calls agent->preUpdate/postUpdate; agent implementation must be correct for these interactions to produce valid behavior

NavMesh.cpp::addNavMeshObstacle calls obstacle->addTo(_tileCache) and the per-frame update loop calls obstacle->preUpdate/postUpdate; obstacle implementation must be correct for tile-cache sync to work
Path following relies on dtPathCorridor and dtNavMeshQuery initialized by navmesh_core
NavMeshQueryHelper wraps dtNavMeshQuery with convenience methods; requires initialized dtNavMesh from navmesh_core
NavMeshCrowdManager allocates a dtCrowd on the dtNavMesh provided by navmesh_core
NavMeshTileManager wraps dtTileCache lifecycle; navmesh_core provides the dtNavMesh for tile updates
Debug utility functions read dtNavMesh tile data and dtCrowd agent states provided by navmesh_core for visualization
sim_world.py imports make_trace from navmesh_contract to construct the trace dict with the correct required keys and flags
navmesh_system.py imports EVENT_TYPES from navmesh_contract to map query type strings to canonical event type names in the emitted events
End-to-end replay validation requires both engine (C++) and game (Python) subsystems to be complete; game_nav_system integration tests exercise the full pipeline after all engine modules are in place
